title: Heart Failure Outcomes with SGLT2 Inhibitors in Adults with Type 2 Diabetes: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
text: A systematic review and meta-analysis reported that SGLT2 inhibitors were associated with lower heart-failure events in adults with type 2 diabetes and cardiorenal risk, supporting the class effect across contemporary studies.

title: Effects of SGLT2 Inhibitors on Clinical Outcomes, Symptoms, Functional Capacity, and Cardiac Remodeling in Heart Failure: A Comprehensive Systematic Review and Multidomain Meta-Analysis of Randomized Trials
text: A multidomain meta-analysis of randomized heart-failure trials found lower cardiovascular death or hospitalization risk with SGLT2 inhibitors and concurrent improvement in symptoms and functional capacity.

title: Post-marketing safety monitoring of tirzepatide: a pharmacovigilance study based on the FAERS database
text: A FAERS-based pharmacovigilance analysis of tirzepatide highlighted gastrointestinal reporting patterns and reinforced the need to interpret spontaneous reports as signal-detection evidence rather than incidence estimates.

title: Orforglipron: A Novel Oral GLP-1 Agonist for the Treatment of Obesity and Diabetes
text: A clinical review summarized the emerging efficacy, tolerability, and strategic positioning of orforglipron as an oral GLP-1 program spanning obesity and type 2 diabetes.

title: Orforglipron, an Oral Small-Molecule GLP-1 Receptor Agonist for Obesity Treatment
text: A phase 3 report described clinically meaningful weight-loss efficacy for oral orforglipron and intensified interest in differentiating late-stage oral GLP-1 programs.

title: Tirzepatide versus Semaglutide Once Weekly in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes
text: The SURPASS-2 trial showed greater HbA1c and weight reduction with tirzepatide than semaglutide 1 mg when added to metformin in adults with type 2 diabetes.

title: Once-weekly tirzepatide versus once-daily insulin degludec as add-on to metformin with or without SGLT2 inhibitors in patients with type 2 diabetes (SURPASS-3): a randomised, open-label, parallel-group, phase 3 trial
text: The SURPASS-3 trial found that once-weekly tirzepatide achieved larger HbA1c and weight reductions than insulin degludec over 52 weeks in insulin-naive adults on metformin with or without SGLT2 inhibitors.

title: Efficacy and safety of once-weekly semaglutide 2.0 mg versus 1.0 mg in patients with type 2 diabetes (SUSTAIN FORTE): a double-blind, randomised, phase 3B trial
text: SUSTAIN FORTE showed that semaglutide 2.0 mg delivered greater HbA1c reduction than semaglutide 1.0 mg, with a similar safety profile in adults with inadequately controlled type 2 diabetes.
